topic: ACL main page 8 Discussion stack-check terminology polish

defect.class: discussion terminology consistency and ACL prose polish
before_text_matches: page 8 line 350: material-risk bins, and embodied smoke tests
defect.reader_effect: The phrase smoke tests was less formal and less consistent with the paper's stack-check terminology.

changes.summary: Changed embodied smoke tests to embodied stack checks in the Discussion opener.

after_evidence.targeted_scan_result: Page 8 renders the Discussion opener with embodied stack checks and no new body-text line-end hyphenation.
after_evidence.full_scan_result: The full after scan reports no body-text line-end hyphenation outside bibliography pages; pages 9-10 retain normal reference-column hyphenation.
after_evidence.visual_result: The page-8 Discussion layout remains stable and the terminology now matches the rest of the claim-gate story.

claim_boundary: Discussion terminology and linebreak behavior only. No metric values, evidence pools, figures, citations, metadata, or supported/forbidden claim scopes were changed.
residual_risk: Bibliography pages retain normal reference-column hyphenation.
